title: Circle and Rectangle Overlapping
description:
You are given a circle represented as (radius, xCenter, yCenter) and an axis-aligned rectangle represented as (x1, y1, x2, y2), where (x1, y1) are the coordinates of the bottom-left corner, and (x2, y2) are the coordinates of the top-right corner of the rectangle.

Return true _if the circle and rectangle are overlapped otherwise return_ false. In other words, check if there is any point (xi, yi) that belongs to the circle and the rectangle at the same time.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def checkOverlap(radius, xCenter, yCenter, x1, y1, x2, y2):
    xClosest = max(x1, min(xCenter, x2))
    yClosest = max(y1, min(yCenter, y2))
    return (xCenter - xClosest) ** 2 + (yCenter - yClosest) ** 2 <= radius ** 2
```
    
    The algorithm finds the closest point from the center of the circle to the rectangle. First, it determines the x-coordinate and y-coordinate of this point `xClosest` and `yClosest`, by finding the minimum and maximum values of x and y coordinates between the center of the circle and the bounds of the rectangle, respectively.

Then, check if the distance squared between the circle's center `(xCenter, yCenter)` and the closest point `(xClosest, yClosest)` is less than or equal to the square of the radius of the circle. If it is, then the circle and the rectangle are overlapping, otherwise they are not.
